_meta.IMPORTANT: These venue clusters were OBSERVED in real data. The Mash/Railway-Greenfield/Ashton-Jubilee gotchas in operator memory belong to the gigs-news feed, NOT KLMA — do not import them here.
_meta.updated: 2026-06-13 — added high-frequency clusters from live resolution (Swiftys, Swan, Moorland Inn, Roebuck)
_meta.ROLE: EVIDENCE + tiny test seed, NOT the dedup mechanism. Spelling/format/apostrophe/spacing variants must be collapsed ALGORITHMICALLY via a slug-strength canonical key (strip apostrophes+punct+case), not listed here. Different-name-same-place cases are resolved by Google place_id + state map + fuzzy (Epic 5), and the exception store is populated by REVIEW/learning, not authored per venue. See architecture/canonicalisation-not-hardcoded-variants.md.

canonicalisationRules: Trim leading/trailing whitespace (rampant in this sheet).
Strip embedded UK postcode (regex: \b[A-Z]{1,2}\d[A-Z\d]?\s*\d[A-Z]{2}\b, case-insensitive) before slug/hash.
Normalise ' in <Town>' and ', <Town>' to a single ', <Town>' form.
Canonicalise BEFORE generating venue external-id slug, BEFORE specialist/multi-act matching, and BEFORE diff keying — otherwise variants create duplicate venues every run.

regionOverride.rule: If the venue string contains a known Cheshire town, set region=Cheshire and city=<that town>; NEVER fall back to defaultCity for an out-of-region venue. Unknown town + no postcode => REVIEW, do not default.

artistRules: Conservative matching (names less unique than venues).
For names ending Band/Duo/Trio/Live/Acoustic/Show, also search the stripped core token (spec artist ladder step 5).
Do NOT strip descriptive suffix from the stored canonical name unless confirmed an alias (e.g. 'Incognito - Party Band' may be the act's real billing).
Same/near name in same region = existing entity (reuse), not a new create.
